The Hymenophyllaceae family is a part of the larger group Pteridophyta, which includes all ferns and their allies. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Chaetostichium minimum include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
